name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI cloud infrastructure company that builds large-scale GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and developer tools for the global AI industry. Formerly Yandex N.V., the company sold its Russian assets for $5.4B in 2024 and rebranded under founder-CEO Arkady Volozh. It operates data centers across Europe and North America, backed by a $2B NVIDIA investment and a landmark $27B, 5-year capacity agreement with Meta Platforms. Subsidiaries include TripleTen (edtech) and Avride (autonomous driving).

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): The transformation from Yandex N.V. — Russia's dominant internet company — into a pure-play Western AI cloud infrastructure provider within 18 months is one of the most dramatic corporate reinventions in recent tech history. Revenue grew from $91.5M in FY2024 to $529.8M in FY2025 (479% YoY), accelerating to 684% YoY in Q1 2026 with $399M quarterly revenue [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS the AI product — it operates GPU-as-a-Service leasing NVIDIA H200, B200 Blackwell, and Rubin architecture chips to enterprises for AI training and inference. 98% of group revenue is AI infrastructure [1]. Market Share (1/2): Nebius is the leading European 'neocloud' and gaining share rapidly, evidenced by the $27B Meta deal ($12B dedicated compute + $15B capacity option) and NVIDIA preferred provider status, but remains small versus AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud [1][2]. Pipeline (1/2): Priority access to NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform, contracted power guidance raised to 4+ GW by end-2026, and CapEx guidance increased to $20-25B from $16-20B for 2027 capacity commitments [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Massive infrastructure investment dwarfs revenue — $20-25B guided 2026 CapEx against $3.0-3.4B revenue guidance reflects extreme R&D/infrastructure intensity [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius articulates a clear purpose of 'building full-stack infrastructure to service the global AI industry,' which is specific and differentiated from hyperscalers. However, the mission is recent (2024 rebrand) and hasn't been validated through multi-decade execution or leadership transition [2]. Community Impact (1/2): The Finland data center utilizes heat recovery to warm local homes — a tangible community benefit — and the company positions itself as enabling 'sovereign-ready' AI cloud for EU healthcare and financial institutions [3]. No specific charitable foundation or community investment programs documented. Employee Retention (1/2): Nebius has aggressively recruited top talent including Dan Lawrence (ex-AWS SVP), Marc Boroditsky (ex-Cloudflare CRO), and John Boynton as Chairman, retaining ~1,300 world-class engineers from the Yandex era [2]. Company is too young in current form for meaningful tenure data. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder-CEO Arkady Volozh maintains a significant ownership stake and demonstrated extraordinary personal commitment by renouncing Russian citizenship and selling $5.4B in Yandex assets to pursue this vision, though no specific open-market purchases in the last 12 months were identified [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Revenue growth is extraordinary — 621.5% YoY per yfinance, accelerating to 684% in Q1 2026 — scoring maximum on both YoY and 5-year growth metrics [1]. D/E of 131.1% scores 0.73 against the 70% target, reflecting debt financing for massive CapEx expansion. ROE of 14.1% is near the 15% target but includes a $584.5M one-time gain in Q2 2025 that inflates trailing margins to 93.1% — operationally the company remains loss-making with negative forward EPS of -$0.27 [5]. Forward P/E is negative (expected losses), scoring 0 on that metric. Forward PEG of 0.63 is excellent, scoring maximum on the growth metric. Note: trailing profit margin of 93.1% is heavily distorted by the Q2 2025 one-time gain; excluding this, the company posted net losses in Q1/Q3/Q4 2025. Weighted calculation yields 8.62/10 = 9. Liquidity passes: $221 x 16.8M daily volume = $3.7B.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure — the company provides the GPU clusters, cloud platform, and developer tools that AI companies use to build and run models. AI disruption is structurally impossible for a company whose entire business is enabling AI; increased AI adoption directly increases demand for Nebius's services [1]. The $27B Meta contract and NVIDIA's $2B investment validate Nebius as foundational infrastructure. The only risk factor preventing a 9/9 score is concentration risk in NVIDIA GPU supply — a shift in GPU architecture or supply constraints could disrupt capacity expansion [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): 98% of group revenue ($390M of $399M in Q1 2026) comes directly from AI infrastructure services, growing 841% YoY. This is the purest AI revenue company outside NVIDIA itself [1].

infinite_game.summary: Nebius under Arkady Volozh demonstrates extraordinary existential flexibility and courage — the decision to sell Yandex's Russian operations, renounce citizenship, and rebuild as a Western AI infrastructure company is one of the most dramatic Just Cause-driven pivots in modern business. However, the company is less than two years old in its current form, which limits evidence of long-term consistency. Sinek would admire the founding story but note that enduring companies prove their infinite mindset through decades, not months.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius's purpose — democratizing AI infrastructure so any company can access the compute needed for AI — is genuine, specific, and validated by Volozh's extraordinary personal sacrifice (renouncing Russian citizenship, divesting $5.4B in Yandex assets) [2]. The cause is service-oriented, idealistic, and big enough to never be fully achieved. However, the company is less than two years old in its current form — the Just Cause has not yet been tested through a market downturn, leadership transition, or multi-decade consistency.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Volozh's decision to abandon Yandex and rebuild from scratch is inherently courageous. The $20-25B CapEx commitment while EBIT-negative, the $27B Meta deal, and the willingness to dilute shareholders through capital raises to fund growth all represent costly long-term bets in service of the cause [1][2]. However, the company has not yet been tested by adversity in its current form — courage is proven in downturns, not bull markets.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The leadership team assembled from top-tier companies (AWS, Cloudflare, NVIDIA ecosystem) suggests a high-talent, mission-driven culture, and the retention of ~1,300 engineers through the Yandex-to-Nebius transformation demonstrates loyalty [2]. The strong engineering culture rooted in designing and operating large-scale platforms is a positive signal. However, the company is too young for meaningful long-term trust evidence — no turnover data, no multi-year cultural track record.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The Nebius transformation is a canonical example of existential flexibility. Volozh and the board sold everything Russian — $5.4B in assets, decades of accumulated market position, Russia's dominant search engine — to pursue a completely new vision as a Western AI infrastructure company [2][3]. This is the Sinek textbook example: a painful, transformative pivot driven by conviction rather than desperation, resulting in a company that went from near-zero revenue to $399M quarterly in under two years.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Nebius explicitly acknowledges hyperscaler strengths — AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud's breadth and scale — while carving out a differentiated 'neocloud' position focused on AI-specific infrastructure [2]. The company's competitive language frames itself as complementary to hyperscalers for AI workloads rather than attempting to beat them across all cloud services. The Meta deal validates this positioning: even the largest hyperscaler customers choose specialized AI infrastructure.

investment_thesis: Nebius is the purest play on AI infrastructure outside of NVIDIA itself — revenue grew 684% YoY to $399M in Q1 2026, Nebius AI adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 45% (from 24% in Q4 2025), and the company reaffirmed FY2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B revenue with ARR targeting $7-9B [1]. The $27B Meta contract ($12B dedicated compute + $15B capacity option) provides unprecedented revenue visibility, NVIDIA's $2B investment and priority access to Vera Rubin architecture provides hardware advantage, and CapEx guidance was raised to $20-25B reflecting customer demand exceeding prior capacity plans [1][2]. Founder-CEO Volozh's extraordinary personal commitment — renouncing Russian citizenship and selling $5.4B in Yandex assets to pursue this vision — provides rare founder-level alignment with long-term shareholders.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on capital intensity and execution risk at unprecedented scale. Nebius is guiding for $20-25B in 2026 CapEx while remaining EBIT-negative on a forward basis (forward EPS of -$0.27), requiring continuous capital raises that dilute shareholders [1][5]. The current $56B market cap at 85x trailing earnings (inflated by a one-time Q2 2025 gain) prices in flawless execution of the $3B+ revenue target. Customer concentration is extreme — Meta represents the single largest contract and any delay or renegotiation would materially impair revenue. The Yandex origin creates lingering ESG and geopolitical perception risk with some institutional investors despite full legal separation from Russia [3]. Building data centers at 4+ GW scale across multiple geographies simultaneously, while managing NVIDIA GPU allocation and power procurement, creates operational execution risk that has not been tested through a downturn.
key_catalysts: FY2026 revenue tracking toward $3.0-3.4B guidance and adjusted EBITDA margin sustaining 40%+ would validate the business model at scale and likely drive institutional investor adoption
NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform priority access in late 2026 provides next-generation hardware advantage that smaller neoclouds cannot match, widening competitive moat
European sovereign cloud expansion — EU data residency regulations create a structural advantage over US hyperscalers, driving enterprise healthcare and financial services contract wins
key_risks: Capital structure risk: $20-25B 2026 CapEx requires ongoing capital raises (debt and equity) that will dilute current shareholders, with forward EPS already negative at -$0.27
Customer concentration: the $27B Meta agreement represents massive single-client dependency; any delay, renegotiation, or cancellation would severely impair revenue guidance and investor confidence

Execution risk at unprecedented scale: building 4+ GW of data center capacity across multiple geographies simultaneously while managing GPU allocation, power procurement, and workforce scaling has never been attempted by a company this young
